plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad market stability.
Trim SMH and QQQ to reduce exposure to downtrending positions.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall invested capital.
Focus on high-quality, lower-volatility assets for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5% per month. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by eliminating leveraged ETFs and reducing concentration. Expect lower volatility, but also limited upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits and manage broad market exposure.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to remove all leveraged ETFs, reducing overall portfolio risk.
Trim QQQI and SMH to reduce exposure to downtrending positions, aligning with risk-first growth.
Buy ZS and ASML, adding high-scoring, uptrending tech and chip exposure.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer to manage potential market swings.
Rebalance the portfolio to blend broad index, quality tech, and controlled risk.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% per month by focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding high-quality, uptrending names. Expect moderate growth with managed drawdown potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits, maintaining broad market exposure.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop, freeing capital for stronger setups.
Trim QQQI and SMH to reduce exposure to downtrending assets.
Buy ZS and NVDA, leaning into high-scoring, uptrending tech and chip names for growth.
Initiate a small position in SOXS to hedge semiconductor exposure.
Maintain a small cash buffer, focusing capital on high-conviction opportunities.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month by stretching for growth. It concentrates in high-scoring setups and uses inverse ETFs for tactical hedging. This approach significantly raises both upside potential and drawdown risk, and leveraged ETFs can decay quickly. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free capital for income-focused assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, prioritizing low drawdown.
Maximize QQQI exposure to 30% for enhanced monthly distributions.
Trim CRWD and QQQ, and sell SMH to reduce high-beta and downtrending positions.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer, prioritizing capital preservation for yield.
Rotate the portfolio into income-generating ETFs for steady monthly distributions.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% per month by prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta exposure, focusing on income and broad index ETFs. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market downturns; this is not a savings account.
